en: Black Friday. Sounds kind of scary. And it was. Black Friday first referred to the collapse of the U.S. gold market in 1869.
A century later, Philadelphia police used Black Friday to describe chaos and congestion.
Downtown streets were clogged with hordes of shoppers headed to the big department stores.
Retailers hated the term, but then tried to reinvent it.
It was the day their profits went from red to black.
So they said Black Friday really started catching on in the 80s 90s pushed by the growth of big box stores.
Today it's all about bargains and Black Friday is dark roots are for the history books.
